theorem.name: Algorithmic Universality Bridge Checklist
theorem.statement: Every polynomial-time SAT solver for the hidden e33-obstructed family must either admit a faithful polynomial-overhead C/V/X sector-interface trace, or exhibit an explicit solver step outside the current integrity interface. If the trace exists and decides the family, then a purely C trace is blocked by the No Public Extractor theorem; a successful trace must therefore contain V or X, and X is an explicit hidden-sector extractor.
theorem.non_claim: This artifact does not prove P != NP. It states the exact obligations needed to promote the existing conditional no-escape result into a full separation claim.

current_posture.reason: The finite C/V/X base and public proof-log classifications are represented as computed or formal consequences, while arbitrary polynomial-time computation remains the open universality tier.

checklist.obligation: Identify the hidden e33-obstructed family as a SAT-complete or polynomially faithful representative family.
checklist.required_artifact: Reduction certificate with source problem, target encoding, size bound, and inverse witness interpretation.
checklist.pass_condition: The reduction has polynomial overhead and preserves yes/no instances without using hidden-sector advice.
checklist.scope_warning: The cycle-8 / Pi_33 residual packet is witnessed as a polynomially faithful representative family. No SAT-complete reduction certificate is present.

checklist.obligation: Define the canonical trace format for solver steps at the C/V/X sector interface.
checklist.required_artifact: Versioned JSON schema for opcode, input state, output state, touched public data, and claimed C/V/X type.
checklist.pass_condition: Every accepted trace event is locally checkable and has deterministic replay semantics.

checklist.obligation: Compile arbitrary polynomial-time solver executions into faithful C/V/X traces with polynomial overhead.
checklist.required_artifact: Compiler theorem or instrumented universal machine with explicit overhead accounting.
checklist.pass_condition: For every polynomial-time solver A, the trace length and event verification cost are polynomial in the input size and preserve A's decision result.
checklist.scope_warning: The compiler emits one universal C trace event per finite public bit-machine instruction with polynomial overhead. Native solvers still require translation into the chosen public bit-machine model.

checklist.obligation: Show every trace event is typed C, V, or X, or record the escaping event as a typed interface defect.
checklist.required_artifact: Total event classifier with fallback residue class for unsupported operations.
checklist.pass_condition: No accepted solver step remains unclassified; unsupported steps are explicit counterexamples to the current interface rather than silent assumptions.
checklist.scope_warning: A finite universal trace vocabulary is totally typed C/V/X with explicit UNCLASSIFIED residue fallback. Native instructions are not silently accepted; they must compile into this vocabulary or become residues.

checklist.obligation: Replay the No Public Extractor theorem over the accepted trace schema.
checklist.required_artifact: Checker showing pure C traces cannot recover or decide the hidden e33 obstruction.
checklist.pass_condition: A trace containing only C events cannot distinguish the obstructed family beyond public-integral consequences.
checklist.scope_warning: Pure-C no-escape is witnessed over the finite universal trace vocabulary: hidden advice, hidden-sector maps, and non-public parity basis recovery are typed X or rejected as residues.

checklist.obligation: Rule out polynomial-size X extractors for the hidden e33 family, or precisely isolate the extractor.
checklist.required_artifact: Lower-bound argument or explicit recovered hidden-sector map.
checklist.pass_condition: Either no polynomial X extractor exists, or the found extractor becomes the new central object to certify.
checklist.scope_warning: The rho_33(gamma_8) hidden-sector target is certified and bare public transport is ruled out, but no polynomial-size X-extractor lower bound is present.

checklist.obligation: Account for V events as visible commutator wall crossings rather than hidden extraction.
checklist.required_artifact: Wall-crossing certificates with public boundary, commutator witness, and replay check.
checklist.pass_condition: V events are visible, replayable, and cannot be silently used as hidden-sector advice.
checklist.scope_warning: Every V class in the finite universal vocabulary has a replayed public-boundary surface certificate; current accepted traces contain no V events, and future V events require instance certificates or become residues.

checklist.obligation: Combine universality, total typing, pure-C no-escape, V accounting, and X lower bound into the final no-escape theorem.
checklist.required_artifact: Machine-checkable dependency ledger linking each obligation to a passing certificate.
checklist.pass_condition: Every polynomial-time deciding solver either violates a closed obligation or yields an explicit X extractor; no silent solver class remains.
checklist.scope_warning: The ledger closes the current accepted trace surface but explicitly blocks full separation on encoded-family completeness and polynomial X-extractor lower bound.

decision_rule.may_claim_prediction: The repository may say it predicts P != NP while these obligations remain open.
decision_rule.may_claim_conditional_theorem: The repository may claim conditional no-escape when the assumptions are named explicitly.
decision_rule.may_claim_full_separation: Only after every checklist item is closed with passing artifacts and the encoded family bridge is certified.

next_highest_yield_item.action: Define and certify a height-coherent/action-return transport that derives rho_33(gamma_8) from edge or circuit data instead of inserting the certified residual scalar.
